meta.title: Thumbs Up for Art and Music — Set 2
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Vocabulary quiz for 'Thumbs Up for Art and Music' — key words and meanings.

questions.question: What does 'art' mean in the context of school?
options: Creating and making things like drawings, paintings, and crafts
questions.explanation: 'Art' is the creative making of things like drawings, paintings, and crafts. In school, art class is where students learn to draw, paint, and create.

questions.question: 'Important' means something does NOT matter very much.
questions.explanation: 'Important' means something matters a lot and should not be ignored. The text repeatedly says art and music are 'important' — they matter a great deal!

questions.question: Art helps you be _______. You can think of new and different ideas.
questions.hint: Able to make new and original ideas
questions.explanation: 'Creative' means able to think of and make new, original things. Art helps you be creative — you come up with your own artistic ideas.

questions.question: What does 'imagination' mean?
options: The ability to think of things that are not real or to think creatively
The ability to do math quickly
questions.explanation: 'Imagination' is the ability to think of things that are not real, or to think creatively. In art, you use your imagination to create new things.

questions.question: What does 'music' mean in the context of school?
options: Singing songs or playing musical instruments
questions.explanation: 'Music' refers to playing instruments or singing. In school music class, students sing songs and learn to play instruments together.

questions.question: Music helps you _______. You must focus and pay close attention to the notes.
questions.hint: To focus all your attention on something
questions.explanation: 'Concentrate' means to focus all your attention. Music requires concentration — you must pay close attention to notes, rhythm, and timing.

questions.question: 'Teamwork' means working alone to finish something.
questions.explanation: 'Teamwork' means working TOGETHER as a group. When you play music with others, you need teamwork — everyone has to work together to make the music sound good.

questions.question: In the text, 'figure out' means what?
options: To solve a problem or find a way to do something
questions.explanation: 'Figure out' means to solve a problem or find how to do something. The text says 'You figure out how to make amazing things' — you solve creative problems in art.

questions.question: Sort these vocabulary words — are they NOUNS (things) or ADJECTIVES/VERBS (actions/descriptions)?
questions.explanation: 'Art,' 'teamwork,' and 'imagination' are things (nouns). 'Creative' (describing word), 'concentrate' (action word), and 'important' (describing word) are adjective/verb forms.

questions.question: The students _______ how to play the song — they worked hard to understand it.
questions.hint: Discovered how to do something through effort
questions.explanation: 'Figured out' means solved or discovered how. When you figure something out, you work through a problem until you understand it.

questions.question: The text says art makes 'amazing things.' What does 'amazing' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Amazing' means very impressive, wonderful, and surprising. In art, you create things that impress and delight people — amazing things!

questions.question: The phrase 'thumbs up' in the title means the author approves and supports art and music.
questions.explanation: 'Thumbs up' is an expression meaning you approve of something or give it a positive sign. The title means the author gives art and music a big approval!

questions.question: When you play music, you have to _______ a lot — you must hold many notes and rhythms in your mind.
questions.hint: To keep information in your mind
questions.explanation: 'Remember' means to keep information in your mind and recall it. Music requires you to remember notes, rhythms, melodies, and lyrics — it trains your memory.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'concentrate' correctly?
options: I concentrate my drawing very good.
You must concentrate when playing music — focus and pay close attention.
The music is concentrate and fun.
questions.explanation: 'Concentrate' means to focus your attention. 'You must concentrate when playing music' correctly uses concentrate as an action — you are doing the concentrating.

questions.question: Sort these skills — are they skills you learn from ART or from MUSIC (according to the text)?
items: how to work as a team

categories.label: Skills from MUSIC
answers: how to work as a team

questions.explanation: Art teaches: creativity, imagination, making amazing things. Music teaches: concentration, teamwork, memory. Both are valuable skills!
